label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. There are five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each has a partner at the start. The question is asking who Claire is dancing with at the end after several partner swaps. Let me try to track each swap step by step. First, let me list out the initial pairings: - Alice is with Lola. - Bob is with Izzi. - Claire is with Sam. - Dave is with Patrick. - Eve is with Karl. So the initial partners are: Alice - Lola Bob - Izzi Claire - Sam Dave - Patrick Eve - Karl Now, the problem describes a series of partner switches. Let me go through each one carefully. First swap: Claire and Bob switch partners. So, Claire was with Sam, and Bob was with Izzi. After swapping, Claire will have Bob's partner (Izzi), and Bob will have Claire's partner (Sam). So after first swap: Alice - Lola Bob - Sam Claire - Izzi Dave - Patrick Eve - Karl Second swap: Claire and Alice switch partners. Claire is currently with Izzi, and Alice is with Lola. Swapping their partners: Claire will now have Alice's partner (Lola), and Alice will have Claire's partner (Izzi). So after second swap: Alice - Izzi Bob - Sam Claire - Lola Dave - Patrick Eve - Karl Third swap: Bob and Claire switch partners. Bob is with Sam, Claire is with Lola. Swapping: Bob gets Claire's partner (Lola), and Claire gets Bob's partner (Sam). After third swap: Alice - Izzi Bob - Lola Claire - Sam Dave - Patrick Eve - Karl Fourth swap: Eve and Bob switch partners. Eve is with Karl, Bob is with Lola. Swapping: Eve gets Bob's partner (Lola), Bob gets Eve's partner (Karl). After fourth swap: Alice - Izzi Bob - Karl Claire - Sam Dave - Patrick Eve - Lola Fifth swap: Dave and Claire switch partners. Dave is with Patrick, Claire is with Sam. Swapping: Dave gets Claire's partner (Sam), Claire gets Dave's partner (Patrick). After fifth swap: Alice - Izzi Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Dave - Sam Eve - Lola So at the end, Claire is dancing with Patrick. The options are (D) Patrick. Let me check again. Let me retrace each step to make sure I didn't make a mistake: Start: A-L, B-I, C-S, D-P, E-K 1. C and B swap partners: C takes I (from B's original partner), B takes S (from C's original partner). So A-L, B-S, C-I, D-P, E-K. 2. C and A swap partners: C (I) and A (L) swap. So A-I, C-L, others same: B-S, D-P, E-K. 3. B and C swap partners: B (S) and C (L) swap. So B-L, C-S, others: A-I, D-P, E-K. 4. E and B swap
